# Test fixture: canonical PRD shard excerpt

Mirrors the post-AF-2026-05-10-2 FR-MTG-10 prose. Positive-control
input for verify-fr-mtg-10-amendment.sh — all checks must pass.

## 4.39 gaia-meeting

- **FR-MTG-10 — Live screen output (within each checkpoint segment)**. The skill MUST stream turn-by-turn to the user's screen with no `>` prefix, **observable at each checkpoint yield** (see FR-MTG-32). The Claude Code skill runtime emits one batched output per LLM turn at the substrate level — "live" therefore means "live within the checkpoint segment that produced these turns", not "live across substrate turn boundaries". Per-turn header MUST show round number, turn number, speaker name, role, per-turn token cost, and running total. After every 10 turns the facilitator MUST emit a brief cost check (cadence counter persists across checkpoint yields per FR-MTG-33 session-state). User-interjection turns MUST be labeled with the user's actual name (resolved from `git config user.name`, overridable via `meeting.user_name` in the project `settings.json`). **AF-2026-05-10-2 amendment — user-as-first-class-attendee path:** when `me`, `user`, or a token resolving to the user's name appears in `/gaia-meeting --invitees`, the user is added as a first-class non-LLM attendee with a turn slot at every yield boundary (post-CHARTER, post-RESEARCH, discuss-cadence, pre-CLOSE, pre-SAVE — composes with FR-MTG-32 amended AskUserQuestion 5-option mapping). The user-token is NOT silently dropped from the resolved invitee CSV (replaces the prior silent-drop / WARNING behavior; supersedes the rolled-back E76-S12/S13 attempts to patch the silent-drop UX). The user's turn slot is offered via the same `AskUserQuestion` substrate primitive that hosts the [c]ontinue / [p]ause / [w]rap-up / [a]bort / [i]nterject options at each yield — the user's [i]nterject free-text response (auto-Other) is emitted as their attendee turn under FR-MTG-10 user-name labeling. The user is NEVER auto-included in the round-robin order between yields (E76-S8 no-fabricated-user-turns invariant preserved with explicit user-as-attendee carve-out per E76-S20 — see SKILL.md §No fabricated user turns + invitee-token enforcement subsections). Session-state gains a `user_attendance: true | false` flag (FR-MTG-33) recording whether the user is an explicitly-invited attendee. Source: intake §"Live screen output"; AF-2026-05-08-1 amendment for substrate-level interactivity gap; AF-2026-05-10-2 amendment for user-as-first-class-attendee design (closes AI-2026-05-09-9, sibling to AF-2026-05-10-1 / E76-S15..S18 in the post-AF-2026-05-09-2-rollback recovery set).
